type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (15-12) is reporting NOx, CO, VOC, PM10, and PM2.5 emissions for 2014. This facility reported NOx and CO emissions for 2013. The following changes occurred from 2013 to 2014, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased by 285%
2) CO emissions increased by 227%
3) VOC emissions increased by 156%
4) PM10 emissions increased by 292%
5) PM2.5 emissions increased by 292%
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in December 2013, which was operational for the entire year in 2014: one (1) 3,550 Caterpillar compressor engine, one (1) 1680 hp Waukesha compressor engine, one (1) 1,000 hp Caterpillar compressor engine and two (2) oil heaters.
2) Flaring volume increased from 25.9 e3m3 to 151.0 e3m3.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (15-12) is reporting NOx, CO and VOC emissions for 2013. This facility reported NOx and CO2 emissions for 2012. The following changes occurred from 2012 to 2013, respectively:
1) NOx emissions decreased from 337.92 to 93.51 tonnes (-72%)
2) CO emissions decreased from 27.41 to 128.51 tonnes (+369%)
3) VOC emissions increased from 9.82 to 29.41 tonnes (+199%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in December 2013: one (1) 3,550 Caterpillar compressor engine, one (1) 1680 hp Waukesha compressor engine, one (1) 1,000 hp Caterpillar compressor engine and two (2) oil heaters.
2) Increased runtime on two (2) 3,550 hp engines for 2013.
3) In 2012, a general EF was used for two (2) 3,550 hp engines, specific model EFs were used for 2013.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (15-12) is reporting NOx, CO and VOC emissions for 2013. This facility reported NOx and CO2 emissions for 2012. The following changes occurred from 2012 to 2013, respectively:
1) NOx emissions decreased from 337.92 to 93.51 tonnes (-72%)
2) CO emissions decreased from 27.41 to 128.51 tonnes (+369%)
3) VOC emissions increased from 9.82 to 29.41 tonnes (+199%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in December 2013: one (1) 3,550 Caterpillar compressor engine, one (1) 1680 hp Waukesha compressor engine, one (1) 1,000 hp Caterpillar compressor engine and two (2) oil heaters.
2) Increased runtime on two (2) 3,550 hp engines for 2013.
3) In 2012, a general EF was used for two (2) 3,550 hp engines, specific model EFs were used for 2013.

comments.comment: Exceeded threshold for 2013.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (15-12) is reporting NOx and CO2 emissions for 2012. This facility reported NOx, CO2, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2011. NOx emissions increased by 125%, CO2 emissions decreased by 74%, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions both increased by 41% from 2011 due to following reasons: The following changes occured from 2011 to 2012, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 150.51 to 337.92 tonnes (+122%)
2) CO2 emissions decreased from 255.89 to 27.41 tonnes (-89%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added two (2) oil heaters
2) Two (2) 3,550 hp engines operated at lean burns in 2012 compared to rich burns in 2011.
3) In 2011, an EF for engines with catalytic converters (CCs) were used for the Waukesha L7042GSI which was operated at a rich burn. This was corrected for 2012, resulting in an increase in NOx and descrease in CO emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The NOx emissions calculations have been reviewed for 2020. We confirm that the reported quantities calculated based on the 2020 Petrinex data as well as equipment specs and operating hours provided by Tourmaline are correct.
Emissions factors were taken from either engine manufacturer’s specification sheets, engine tests, or published documents by Clearstone Engineering Ltd.
